**Air Côte d'Ivoire** is the [flag carrier](/wiki/Flag_carrier "Flag carrier") of [Ivory Coast](/wiki/Ivory_Coast "Ivory Coast"),[[4]](#cite_note-West_Africa’s_Air_Cote_d’Ivoire_gets_connected-4) based in [Abidjan](/wiki/Abidjan "Abidjan"). The company succeeded the country's former flag carrier [Air Ivoire](/wiki/Air_Ivoire "Air Ivoire"),[[5]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_plans_Oct-2012_launch_with_international_network_of_nine_cities-5) which went bankrupt in 2011.[[6]](#cite_note-Air_Cote_d'Ivoire_To_Launch_With_Two_Airbuses-6) It started operations on 12 November 2012.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The airline was set up on 15 May 2012,[[7]](#cite_note-The_airline_Air_Côte_d'Ivoire_becomes_reality-7) as a private-public entity, partially owned by [Air France](/wiki/Air_France "Air France") – which intended to make [Port Bouet Airport](/wiki/Port_Bouet_Airport "Port Bouet Airport") a regional [hub](/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub")[[8]](#cite_note-Air_France_revisits_plans_for_Ivory_Coast_airline_partnership-8) – and the [Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development](/wiki/Aga_Khan_Fund_for_Economic_Development "Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development") (AKFED).[[9]](#cite_note-New_Ivory_Coast_flag_carrier_set_for_take-off-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_ne_ressemblera_pas_à_Air_Ivoire-10) Air Côte d'Ivoire had an initial capital of [CFAF](/wiki/CFA_franc "CFA franc")2.5 billion, majority-owned by the [government of Ivory Coast](/wiki/Government_of_Ivory_Coast "Government of Ivory Coast") (65%), with the balance held by [Air France Finance](/wiki/Air_France "Air France") (20%) and Aérienne de Participation-Côte d'Ivoire (15%), an airline holding company of AKFED.[[7]](#cite_note-The_airline_Air_Côte_d'Ivoire_becomes_reality-7) The Ivory Coast had had no national carriers since the collapse of [Air Ivoire](/wiki/Air_Ivoire "Air Ivoire") in March 2011 (2011-03).[[11]](#cite_note-New_airline_in_Ivory_Coast_to_launch_in_July-11)

The airline will have "technical, commercial and operational synergies" with [Air Mali](/wiki/Air_Mali_(2005) "Air Mali (2005)") and [Air Burkina](/wiki/Air_Burkina "Air Burkina"), two other airlines associated with the AKFED.[[11]](#cite_note-New_airline_in_Ivory_Coast_to_launch_in_July-11)[[12]](#cite_note-New_national_Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_plans_domestic_and_international_route_launches_this_year-12)

As of November 2012, it was planned for the new company to have 13 flight captains, 12 first officers and 37 cabin crew.[[13]](#cite_note-New_Ivory_Coast_Air_Cote_d`Ivoire_set_to_launch-13) The company projects to have a passenger capacity of 330,000 per year.[[14]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire/Les_premiers_vols_régionaux_en_juillet_2012-14) It carried 253,000 passengers during 2013.[[15]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire:_253_000_passagers_en_2013-15)

## Corporate affairs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Corporate affairs")]

### Ownership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ownership")]

As of April 2017[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit), the parent entities of the shareholders are:[[16]](#cite_note-Guinea-16)

| Shareholder | Interest |
| --- | --- |
| [Government of Ivory Coast](/wiki/Government_of_Ivory_Coast "Government of Ivory Coast") | 58% |
| [Air France-KLM](/wiki/Air_France-KLM "Air France-KLM") | 011% |
| Golden Road | 023% |
| Other investors | 08% |
| **Total** | **100%** |

The [Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development](/wiki/Aga_Khan_Fund_for_Economic_Development "Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development") was a 15% shareholder until 2013, when it was reported that it would withdraw from Air Côte d'Ivoire.[[17]](#cite_note-Aga_Khan_Fund_to_Pull_Out_of_Ivory_Coast_Airline_in_Capital_Move-17) The shareholding was acquired by Golden Road, a consortium of private Ivorian investors,[[18]](#cite_note-Golden-18) with shareholdings changing following subsequent fundraising.

### Key people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Key people")]

As of November 2025[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit), Air Côte d'Ivoire's [CEO](/wiki/CEO "CEO") is Laurent Loukou.[[27]](#cite_note-Embraer_Secures_Air_Cote_d’Ivoire,_Helvetic_Orders_At_Dubai_Airshow-28)

## Destinations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Destinations")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4a/TUTSV-FOOL1.jpg/250px-TUTSV-FOOL1.jpg)](/wiki/File:TUTSV-FOOL1.jpg)

Air Côte d'Ivoire [Airbus A320-200](/wiki/Airbus_A320-200 "Airbus A320-200") at [Léon-Mba International Airport](/wiki/L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport "Léon-Mba International Airport") in [Libreville](/wiki/Libreville "Libreville")

As of September 2012[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit), the airline had initial plans to operate scheduled services from its hub in Abidjan on a network covering nine international destinations in [West](/wiki/West_Africa "West Africa") and [East Africa](/wiki/East_Africa "East Africa"), although some of them would be flown by sister companies [Air Burkina](/wiki/Air_Burkina "Air Burkina") and [Air Mali](/wiki/Air_Mali_(2005) "Air Mali (2005)"). Cities served with Air Côte d'Ivoire's own aircraft were expected to include [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"), [Conakry](/wiki/Conakry "Conakry"), [Cotonou](/wiki/Cotonou "Cotonou") and [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar").[[5]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_plans_Oct-2012_launch_with_international_network_of_nine_cities-5) Operations commenced on 12 November 2012 (2012-11-12),[[28]](#cite_note-Ivory_Coast's_National_Carrier_Takes_Off-29) with the [carrier](/wiki/Air_carrier "Air carrier")'s [maiden flight](/wiki/Maiden_flight "Maiden flight") linking Abidjan with [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar").[[29]](#cite_note-Premiers_vols_commerciaux_pour_Air_Côte_d’Ivoire-30)

As of July 2024[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit), the company's route map indicate that it serves the following destinations:[[30]](#cite_note-reg2407-31)

### Fleet strategy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Air_C%C3%B4te_d%27Ivoire&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Fleet strategy")]

The carrier took delivery of its first aircraft, an ex-Air France [Airbus A319](/wiki/Airbus_A319 "Airbus A319") manufactured in 2004, in October 2012 (2012-10) on lease from [Macquarie AirFinance](/wiki/Macquarie_Group "Macquarie Group").[[50]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_leases_A319_from_Macquarie_AirFinance-51) During the [2013 Dubai Air Show](/wiki/Dubai_Air_Show "Dubai Air Show"), it was announced the carrier placed a conditional order for up to four [Bombardier Q400s](/wiki/Bombardier_Q400 "Bombardier Q400").[[51]](#cite_note-Bombardier_and_Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_Sign_Agreement_for_up_to_Four_Q400_NextGen_Aircraft-52)[[52]](#cite_note-DUBAI:_Air_Cote_D'Ivoire_conditionally_orders_two_Q400s-53) Worth [US$](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar")69 million,[[nb 1]](#cite_note-55) a firm order was announced in December 2013 (2013-12).[[54]](#cite_note-Air_Cote_d’Ivoire_firms_Bombardier_Q400_order-56)[[55]](#cite_note-Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_Firms_Bombardier_Q400_Order-57)[[56]](#cite_note-Bombardier_and_Air_Côte_d’Ivoire_Reach_Firm_Purchase_Agreement_with_Options_for_up_to_Four_Q400_NextGen_Aircraft-58) [African Export-Import Bank](/wiki/African_Export-Import_Bank "African Export-Import Bank") will finance 95% of the acquisition.[[53]](#cite_note-Air_Cote_d'Ivoire_agrees_$114_mln_aircraft_loan_from_Afreximbank-54) The carrier plans to use one of these aircraft to replace the [E170](/wiki/E-170 "E-170") on regional services.[[57]](#cite_note-Air_Cote_d’Ivoire_plots_growth_course-59) The first Dash 8-Q400 was delivered in late 2014.[[58]](#cite_note-60) An additional order for two more aircraft of the type was placed in April 2015 (2015-04).[[59]](#cite_note-Air_Cote_d'Ivoire_orders_two_more_Bombardier_Q400s-61)

An order comprising two [Airbus A320neos](/wiki/Airbus_A320neo "Airbus A320neo") and two [Airbus A320ceos](/wiki/Airbus_A320ceo "Airbus A320ceo") that was placed in April 2016 (2016-04) was boosted in July the same year when an additional A320neo was ordered.[[60]](#cite_note-Ivory_Coast_carrier_adds_an_A320neo-62)[[61]](#cite_note-Ivory_Coast_carrier_orders_two_Airbus_A320ceos,_two_A320neos-63) Air Côte d'Ivoire took delivery of their first [Airbus A330-900](/wiki/Airbus_A330-900 "Airbus A330-900") in early September 2025.[[47]](#cite_note-FirstA330neo-48) In November 2025, Air Côte d'Ivoire placed an order for four [Embraer 175s](/wiki/Embraer_E-Jet_family "Embraer E-Jet family") with options for eight more aircraft of the type, to expand domestic and regional routes.[[27]](#cite_note-Embraer_Secures_Air_Cote_d’Ivoire,_Helvetic_Orders_At_Dubai_Airshow-28)[[48]](#cite_note-:0-49)
